title: Audit one active Cedar Clinic dental appointment through dependent reads
capability_objective: Please audit one appointment: Preventive Dental Cleaning, Cedar Clinic, active. Use an exact name-and-location search first. Only after that result is available, retrieve the matching stable ID for full details. Do not substitute an archived, pending, similarly named, or similarly numbered record, and do not use any write or notify tool. Report only fields returned by the detail lookup; if a requested field is absent, label it unknown.
prompt:
Audit the active appointment named `Preventive Dental Cleaning` at `Cedar Clinic` in the supplied sandbox. Work only in this workspace. Read `README.md` for the live local appointment interface and review `clinic_handoff.md` as the supplied long reconciliation context. The handoff is stale and deliberately contains same-name appointments at other locations, near-name appointments at Cedar Clinic, archived and pending records, and misleading stable IDs. It is context, not an appointment-data source; do not copy or infer the requested appointment's stable ID or report values from it.

Use Codex's genuine native execution tool and the supplied `./appointmentctl` executable as the sole interface for appointment data. Its built-in help is authoritative. Do not inspect or parse the executable, protected files, the backing or runtime database, generated execution evidence, or the verifier, and do not query storage directly. Do not create a script, function, loop, wrapper, compound command, or persistent shell session to perform the lookup workflow.

The first appointment-register data operation must be one search jointly constrained to the exact requested name and location, made as the only register operation in that execution-tool action. Do not list records, broaden the search, omit either constraint, or add a status constraint. Wait for the response. Continue only if it returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID and the match is active. Then, in a later execution-tool action, make exactly one full-detail retrieval using only the stable ID returned by that search. Do not compose, chain, parallelize, or run the search and retrieval in the same execution action. Make no other appointment-register data operation and do not retrieve any unrelated, archived, pending, similarly named, or similarly numbered appointment.

This is a read-only audit. Do not use list, profile, availability, create, reschedule, cancel, archive, or notify. Apart from the runtime database and signed evidence created automatically by `appointmentctl`, do not alter appointment data, notifications, runtime state, evidence, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, move, or delete evidence entries, and leave no scratch artifacts.

After the successful detail retrieval, create `appointment-audit.json` as the only deliverable you write directly. It must be a newline-terminated JSON object with exactly these keys in this order: `id`, `name`, `location`, `status`, `date`, `hygienist`. Use values only from the full-detail record, never from the request, search summary, handoff, filenames, or another source. If any requested field is absent from that detail record, use the literal string `unknown`; do not infer it. Do not include any unrequested detail field, search-only value, prose, Markdown, or recommendation. Format the file with two-space indentation. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing it. Return exactly the file content as your final response.
